patch.value: Close-time completeness signal for structured resolution fields (resolution/expected/actual) — config-gated warn or require

## Problem
`validate_resolution_missing_fields` keeps creeping (269 → 272 across 2026-07-09→10) even though `close_reason` is enforced: agents close items with a reason but skip the structured triple `resolution` / `expected_result` / `actual_result` (set via `pm close --resolution/--expected/--actual` or `pm update`). pm-e9zh backfills the pre-enforcement legacy set, but nothing stops NEW closes from adding to the worklist, so the backfill target moves.

## Proposal
1. Emit a completeness line in the `pm close` envelope when any of the three structured fields is absent (always on, one short line — token-cheap, mirrors the existing next_transition hint pattern).
2. Add a governance settings key (e.g. `close.require_resolution_fields: off | warn | require`, default `warn`) so strict workspaces can hard-gate like `require_close_reason` already does.
3. Contract + docs + completion wiring per the new-flag checklist; validate check text should point at the setting.

## Acceptance criteria
- Closing without the triple prints exactly one advisory line naming the missing fields and the flags to set them; with `require`, close exits non-zero with the same recovery bundle shape as close_reason_required.
- `validate_resolution_missing_fields` stops growing on workspaces with warn/require enabled (verified in e2e fixture).
- No output change when all three fields are provided.

## Relation
related pm-e9zh (legacy backfill) — this item is the faucet, e9zh is the mop.

Advisory line on close when resolution/expected/actual missing; require mode exits non-zero with recovery bundle; e2e fixture proves drift stops under warn/require; zero output change when fields provided

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Evidence 2026-07-10: validate_resolution_missing_fields drifted 269 -> 272 (+3) in one day — the 2026-07-09 closes of pm-2ldo, pm-q6gx, pm-cu1i (all closed via close -m with strong prose close_reason but no structured resolution/expected/actual). Confirms the faucet is every routine agent close, not a legacy backlog: without a close-time signal the structured fields will keep losing to close_reason. Rate data point for prioritization: ~3/day at current agent throughput.
